ddls:
CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement entry */,
  "us_value" REAL /* Measurement value in US units */,
  "uk_value" REAL /* Measurement value in UK units */,
  "euro_value" REAL /* Measurement value in European units */,
  "in_value" REAL /* Measurement value in Indian units */,
  "cm_value" TEXT /* Measurement value in centimeters */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Type of unit (e.g., inches, cm, kg) */,
  "measurement_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement was taken */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement type */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Validation status of the measurement */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method used for the measurement */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES measurement_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the measurement type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the measurement type */,
  "unit_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol of the measurement type */,
  "conversion_to_standard" REAL /* Conversion factor to a standard unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "conversion_factors" (
  "conversion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conversion factor */,
  "from_unit" TEXT /* Unit from which conversion is made */,
  "to_unit" TEXT /* Unit to which conversion is made */,
  "factor" REAL /* The conversion factor used between the two units */,
  "standard_unit" TEXT /* Standard unit to which the conversion is made */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the conversion factor was last updated */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conversion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conversion_factors_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES measurement_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_conversion_factors_from_unit FOREIGN KEY ("from_unit") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source (e.g., laboratory name) */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the source */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the data source */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the data source */,
  "established_date" TEXT /* Date when the source was established */,
  "reliability_score" REAL /* Reliability score of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement that was updated */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who made the update */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date of the update */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the changes made */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement type */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Validation status of the log entry */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method used for the measurement in the log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurement_logs_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES measurement_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurement_logs_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES measurement_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each measurement entry
Type of unit (e.g., inches, cm, kg)
